financial.note: Holds ~31.74% stake; the primary vehicle for the Teawsomboonkij family's control over strategic capital allocation.
Led by Winai Teawsomboonkij (~17.94% direct stake); the family maintains over 50% combined voting power.

financial.entity: The Bank of New York (Nominees) Limited
financial.note: Significant institutional holding (~13.98%), reflecting TFG's inclusion in global emerging market and agri-food indices.

supply_chain.note: Primary growth driver for 2026; expanding from 615 to 850 branches. Aims to contribute 50% of total revenue to hedge against meat price volatility.
Raw material costs for feed mill operations; TFG has locked in ~60-70% of 2026 input costs to stabilize margins.
Vietnam remains a key growth engine; 2026 capacity expansion focuses on integrating animal feed mills with poultry and swine farms.

equity_cross_holdings.entity: Thai Foods Feed Mill Co., Ltd.
Thai Foods Swine Farm Co., Ltd.

competitors.note: Dominant rival in all segments (poultry, swine, feed); CPF's global scale pressures TFG's pricing power in export markets.
Direct competitor in the premium meat and retail shop segments ('Betagro Deli' vs 'Thai Foods Fresh Market').
Rival specifically in the frozen and processed chicken export segment.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The 'Price Maker' factor; 2026 swine prices in Thailand are projected at THB 70/kg, driving significant margin expansion.
Directly impacts overseas earnings; full-year 2026 prices are targeted at VND 55,000–60,000/kg.
Exports (chicken) are USD-denominated; a weaker THB boosts revenue but increases the cost of imported feed ingredients.
Retail expansion is labor-intensive; 2026 wage hikes impact the SG&A of the 850-branch Fresh Market network.
